# JVtools

> Houdini digital assets and tools for artists and technical directors. Pick a product below for its documentation.

- [JVtools](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/index.md): JVtools
- [Reallusion Importer for Houdini](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/readme.md): Welcome! Reallusion Importer for Houdini turns a Character Creator 5 or iClone 8 character into a fully shaded, animatable, render-ready character in Houdini's Solaris environment — in seconds instead of hours.
- [RPLidar In](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/readme.md): RPLidar In brings live Slamtec RPLIDAR point-cloud data straight into Houdini as SOP geometry. Plug in the sensor, drop the node, and every rotation of the spinning laser becomes a fresh ring of points you can feed into simulations, visualizations, or interactive installations — in real time.

## Reallusion Importer for Houdini

### Getting Started

- [Requirements & Installation](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/getting-started/installation.md): Before you start, make sure you have:
- [USD vs FBX](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/getting-started/import-modes.md): Version 1.2 added a second way to import your character. At the very top of the node is an Import dropdown with two choices — USD (the default) and FBX.
- [Quick Start](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/getting-started/quick-start.md): This page gets you from a fresh Houdini scene to a shaded, rendered character in about five steps. We'll keep it simple here — every control is explained in depth later in the Using the Asset section.
- [Preparing Your Character in Character Creator](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/getting-started/preparing-your-character.md): This is the most important setup page in the documentation. Getting your Character Creator export settings right is the difference between a character that imports perfectly and one that's missing textures, wrinkles, or hair detail.

### Using the Asset

- [The Lookdev Controller](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/lookdev-controller.md): When you click Build Character, the tool creates a bright red controller node in the LOP network (Solaris). This node is the heart of the whole
- [Skin](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/skin.md): The Skin folder on the lookdev controller fine-tunes how your character's skin reads. The tool already builds physically-based skin with subsurface scattering, normal detail, and proper roughness — these controls let you push it toward your taste or your shot's lighting.
- [Skin Fix](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/skin-fix.md): Character Creator outfits don't always sit perfectly on every body, so you'll sometimes see a sleeve, collar, or boot poking through the skin. Skin Fix
- [Wrinkles](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/wrinkles.md): One of the tool's standout features is its animated expression-wrinkle system. Character Creator faces have a sophisticated wrinkle system — as a character frowns, squints, or opens their mouth, fine wrinkles appear in the right places.
- [Displacement](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/displacement.md): Displacement adds real, fine surface relief to your character — skin pores, deep wrinkles, fabric weave — by physically pushing the geometry around at render time, based on displacement maps.
- [Eyes](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/eyes.md): The Eyes folder gives you deep control over your character's eyes — recreating much of Character Creator's HD eye look, plus stylized glow effects. The controls are organized into tabs: Iris, Limbus, Sclera, Eye Shadow, and Wetness, with the Glow controls below.
- [Hair](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/hair.md): The Hair folder is one of the tool's most powerful features: a complete hair re-dye system. You can recolor any compatible character's hair — change it from brown to silver, add ombré fades, paint in highlight streaks — all without going back to Character Creator.
- [Animation](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/animation.md): The tool can play the motion baked into your character's FBX, and it can also manage a small library of additional animation clips that you add from other FBX files — letting you switch between different motions on the same character.
- [Rendering](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/using/rendering.md): The tool builds your character's materials specifically for Karma XPU, Houdini's GPU-accelerated production renderer. This page covers how to get your character on screen and the optional one-click render setup.

### Reference

- [Limitations & Expectations](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/reference/limitations.md): This page is here to set honest expectations before and after you buy. The tool does a lot, but Character Creator's FBX export — and Houdini itself — impose some hard limits that no importer can work around.
- [Performance & Caching](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/reference/performance.md): Character Creator characters are detailed, and detailed characters use memory. This page explains where the cost comes from and how to keep your sessions fast and lean.
- [Troubleshooting & FAQ](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/reference/troubleshooting.md): Common issues and their fixes. Most import problems trace back to either an export setting or a moved file.
- [Changelog](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/reference/changelog.md): USD import mode
- [License](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/reallusion-importer/reference/license.md): This is the End User License Agreement for the Reallusion Importer for Houdini. By downloading, installing, or using the asset, you agree to these terms.

## RPLidar In

- [Getting Started](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/getting-started.md): Copy the RPLidar_In.hdalc file into your Houdini otls directory, or use File → Import → Houdini Digital Asset and point it at the file. It installs the SOP node type RPLidar In.
- [Hardware & Cabling](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/hardware.md): RPLidar In was developed against the Slamtec RPLIDAR A2M12 — a 360°, 16 m range, spinning laser scanner. Other A2-family units should behave the same. The sensor stays completely silent until Houdini commands the motor to spin, so no lights or sound when you first plug it in is normal.
- [Modes](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/modes.md): The Mode parameter is the master switch for the node. It has three settings.
- [Recording & Playback](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/recording.md): Recording captures the live stream to disk so you can replay it later — develop offline, iterate on a solver against a repeatable take, or archive an installation.
- [Visualization](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/visualization.md): The Visualize folder draws optional on-screen guides so you can see the sensor, its range, and its beams. All guides are extra geometry tagged with the point/primitive group rplidar_viz.
- [Crop](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/crop.md): The Crop folder culls scan points that fall outside a rectangle on the ground, so only points inside the rectangle reach downstream nodes. This is useful
- [Live Simulation (POP Network)](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/live-sim.md): RPLidar In's point cloud is ordinary SOP geometry, so it can feed any solver — POPs, Vellum, or your own network. The Create POP Network button sets up a working example for you in one click.
- [Output & Attributes](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/output.md): RPLidar In has two outputs and no inputs.
- [Parameter Reference](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/parameters.md): Every parameter on the RPLidar In node, grouped by function. Names in code are the internal parameter names.
- [Troubleshooting](https://tonehammer.github.io/jvtools-docs/rplidar-in/troubleshooting.md): Press Test Sensor. It prints the sensor's identity, health, and available scan modes. No response means Houdini can't talk to the sensor.
